title: Largest Subarray with Sum 0

content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array of integers arrarrarr which may contain both positive and negative values.

He is curious to know the length of the **longest contiguous subarray** whose sum is equal to 000.

Your task is to help Chef find this length.

If no such subarray exists, output 000.

Function DeclarationFunction NamelargestSubarrayWithSumZerolargestSubarrayWithSumZerolargestSubarrayWithSumZero – This function finds the length of the longest contiguous subarray with sum equal to zero.

Parameters
arrarrarr : An array of integers (can contain positive and negative values).
Return Value
Returns a single integer — the **maximum length** of a contiguous subarray whose sum is 000.
Returns 000 if no such subarray exists.
Constraints
1≤n≤1061 \leq n \leq 10^61≤n≤106
−103≤arr[i]≤103−10^3 \leq arr[i] \leq 10^3−103≤arr[i]≤103
Array elements can be positive, negative, or zero

Input Format

The first line of each test case contains a single integer nnn — the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains nnn space-separated integers arr[i]arr[i]arr[i] — the elements of the array.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the length of the longest subarray whose sum is `0`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array; the second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line – the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum equals 0. Print 0 if no such subarray exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The subarray from index 3 to 6 ([-2, 2, 6, -6]) sums to 0 and its length 4 is maximal.
The whole array sums to 0, therefore the longest zero‑sum subarray has length 5.
There is no subarray with sum 0.

solution.approach: For each prefix sum of the array we store the earliest index where this sum occurs in a hash map. If the same prefix sum appears again at index j, the subarray (previous_index+1 … j) has sum 0 and its length is j‑previous_index. Keeping the maximum length over all such repetitions yields the answer. Initialise the map with sum 0 at index -1 to handle subarrays that start at index 0. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) additional memory for the map.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the hash map storing first occurrences of each prefix sum.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
        print(0)
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
        print(RINT(-1000, 1000))
    # Add further generation logic for other indices as needed.
